title: Record: TTT LoRA + SOTA Training (10L MuonWD FP16Emb OvertoneInit)
body:
## Summary

Stacks LoRA test-time training evaluation onto the current SOTA training recipe. These two improvements are orthogonal and have not been combined in any prior submission:

- **Training**: 10L, Muon WD (0.02), FP16 tied embed, overtone spectral SVD init, phase-transition resid_mix, sliding window eval stride=64 (from `2026-03-19_SlidingWindow_FP16Emb_10L_MuonWD_OvertoneInit`, val_bpb=1.1748)
- **Evaluation**: Per-document LoRA adaptation (rank=8 on Q/V + LM head), document-isolated chunked evaluation with Adam optimizer (from `2026-03-17_LoRA_TTT`, val_bpb=1.1928 on baseline)

The TTT ablation showed ~0.037 bpb improvement from eval-time adaptations applied to the weaker baseline model. Applying the same technique to the SOTA-trained model is expected to yield ~1.14 val_bpb.

## Changes

- Added TTT hyperparameters to `Hyperparameters` class
- Modified `CausalSelfAttention.forward`, `Block.forward`, `GPT.forward` to accept optional LoRA delta parameters (no-op when not provided, preserving training behavior)
- Added `BatchedLinearLoRA`, `BatchedTTTLoRA` classes for batched per-document LoRA adaptation
- Added `eval_val_ttt_lora` function for document-isolated chunked LoRA evaluation
- After standard sliding window eval, runs TTT LoRA eval and reports both scores

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify training reproduces ~1.1748 val_bpb on standard sliding window eval (identical training path)
- [ ] Verify TTT LoRA eval produces improvement over standard eval
- [ ] Verify total eval time (sliding window + TTT) stays under 10 minutes
- [ ] Run 3 seeds for statistical significance (p < 0.01)
- [ ] Verify compressed artifact stays under 16MB

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
